pattern: DP tracking whether the next element is added positively or negatively within the current subarray, with the option to start a new subarray resetting the sign.
hints: The cost of a subarray alternates signs starting with +: the first element is positive, the second negative, the third positive, etc. So odd-indexed elements within a subarray are subtracted.
Use DP with two states: pos (current element is added with + sign) and neg (current element is subtracted). You can always start a fresh subarray, resetting to the + state.
Transitions: pos[i] = max(neg[i-1], pos[i-1]) + nums[i] (either continue from neg state or start fresh); neg[i] = pos[i-1] - nums[i] (must come from the preceding + element).

edge_inputs.description: all positive — take whole array as one subarray
alternating sign array — splitting produces gains
large negative at position 2 — splitting avoids subtracting it
all negatives — only first element of each single-element subarray is taken, but that's still negative; can we do better?
alternating positive/negative — maximizing by grouping pairs

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maximumTotalCost(self, nums) -> int:
        # Brute force O(n^2): try all possible split points
        # Actually need to enumerate all subarray partitions — too slow for large n.
        # Use DP oracle instead (different formulation)
        n = len(nums)
        if n == 0:
            return 0
        # dp_pos[i]: max cost when nums[i] is added with + sign
        # dp_neg[i]: max cost when nums[i] is added with - sign
        INF = float('-inf')
        dp_pos = INF
        dp_neg = INF
        dp_pos = nums[0]
        # There is no valid neg state at index 0 (can't start a subarray with -)
        for i in range(1, n):
            new_pos = max(dp_pos, dp_neg) + nums[i]  # start new subarray or continue from neg
            new_neg = dp_pos - nums[i]               # extend the subarray (flip sign)
            dp_pos, dp_neg = new_pos, new_neg
        return max(dp_pos, dp_neg)
